title: DBMS Connector Overview
text: DBMS Connector Overview This section provides an overview of supported functionality and limitations for all DBMS connectors. Each Uniface database connector is identified in Uniface by a 3-character mnemonic, and each one has its own set of limits, SQL scripts and utilities, and properties that you can configure using the DBMS options provided. For the supported platforms and product versions for your DBMS, refer to the Platform Availability Matrix . The connector mnemonic is used in assignment files to designate the connector version, the path to the connector, and the options to be used. For more information, see [DRIVER_SETTINGS] and Paths and Path Assignments . Table 1. Connector Mnemonics Connector Mnemonic Database DB2 DB2. For more information, see DB2 . INF Informix. For more information, see Informix . LDP LDAP Connector. For more information, see LDAP Connector . MSS Microsoft SQL Server. For more information, see Microsoft SQL Server . MQL MySQL. For more information, see MySQL . OD0, OD1, OD2 ODBC. For more information, see ODBC . ORA Oracle. For more information, see Oracle . PGS PostgreSQL. For more information, see PostgreSQL . SEQ Sequential. For more information, see Sequential . SLE SQLite. For more information, see SQLite . SYB Sybase. For more information, see Sybase . TXT Text. For more information, see TXT .
